2603.25269.title: When Hate Meets Facts: LLMs-in-the-Loop for Check-worthiness Detection in Hate Speech
2603.25269.paper_summary: Hateful content online is often expressed using fact-like, not necessarily correct information, especially in coordinated online harassment campaigns and extremist propaganda. Failing to jointly address hate speech (HS) and misinformation can deepen prejudice, reinforce harmful stereotypes, and expose bystanders to psychological distress, while polluting public debate. Moreover, these messages require more effort from content moderators because they must assess both harmfulness and veracity, i.e., fact-check them. To address this challenge, we release WSF-ARG+, the first dataset which combines hate speech with check-worthiness information. We also introduce a novel LLM-in-the-loop framework to facilitate the annotation of check-worthy claims. We run our framework, testing it with 12 open-weight LLMs of different sizes and architectures. We validate it through extensive human evaluation, and show that our LLM-in-the-loop framework reduces human effort without compromising the annotation quality of the data. Finally, we show that HS messages with check-worthy claims show significantly higher harassment and hate, and that incorporating check-worthiness labels improves LLM-based HS detection up to 0.213 macro-F1 and to 0.154 macro-F1 on average for large models.
2603.25269.paper_summary_zh: 在線上仇恨內容通常以類似事實的方式表達，但不一定正確，特別是在協調的網路騷擾活動和極端主義宣傳中。<lb/>未能共同解決仇恨言論（HS）和錯誤資訊可能會加深偏見，強化有害的刻板印象，並使旁觀者面臨心理困擾，同時污染公共辯論。<lb/>此外，這些訊息需要內容審核者付出更多努力，因為他們必須評估有害性和真實性，即進行事實檢查。<lb/>為了解決這一挑戰，我們發布了WSF-ARG+，這是第一個將仇恨言論與可檢查性資訊結合的數據集。<lb/>我們還引入了一種新穎的LLM-in-the-loop框架，以促進可檢查主張的標註。<lb/>我們運行我們的框架，並用12個不同大小和架構的開放權重LLM進行測試。<lb/>我們通過廣泛的人類評估來驗證它，並顯示我們的LLM-in-the-loop框架在不妨礙數據標註質量的情況下減少了人類的努力。<lb/>最後，我們顯示帶有可檢查主張的HS訊息顯示出顯著更高的騷擾和仇恨，並且納入可檢查性標籤使基於LLM的HS檢測平均提高了0.213的宏觀F1和0.154的宏觀F1，對於大型模型而言。
2603.25269.author: Nicolás Benjamín Ocampo et.al.
2603.25269.authors: Nicolás Benjamín Ocampo, Tommaso Caselli, Davide Ceolin
